description_long: This object is a vintage, industrial-style camera with a robust metallic body and a bellows-style lens extension. The camera features a large, circular flash reflector mounted on an articulated arm, a pressure gauge, and several cylindrical canisters attached to its sides, suggesting a complex mechanical or pneumatic system. The front face displays a large lens surrounded by a gear-like ring, and there are various knobs, dials, and levers for manual adjustments. The surface is predominantly metallic bronze with areas of wear and patina, and there are colored labels and a gauge visible on one side. The camera's design is reminiscent of early 20th-century field or scientific cameras, with a utilitarian and rugged appearance. Its size and construction indicate it is a heavy, stationary device likely used for specialized photography or documentation in urban or industrial environments.
description: A vintage, industrial camera with a bellows lens, large flash reflector, pressure gauge, and multiple metallic canisters, finished in bronze with visible wear and colored labels.
description_view_0: Side view showing the bellows lens extension, pressure gauge, and cylindrical canisters, with the flash reflector arm visible above.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the large lens, gear-like ring, and main control dials, with the flash reflector and canisters flanking the body.
description_view_2: Opposite side view focusing on the pressure gauge, bellows lens, and the articulated flash reflector arm.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying colored labels, a large circular component, and the flash reflector arm, with canisters and tubing visible.
